title_en: The Met’s “Costume Art” Makes a Case for Fashion
title_zh: 大都会艺术博物馆的“服装艺术”展为时尚辩护

paragraphs.en: The Met’s “Costume Art” Makes a Case for Fashion
paragraphs.zh: 大都会艺术博物馆的“服装艺术”展为时尚辩护

paragraphs.en: From its new galleries off the museum’s Great Hall, the Costume Institute seeks to put clothing at the center of art history.
paragraphs.zh: 从博物馆大礼堂旁边的新展厅，服装研究所希望把服装放在艺术史的核心位置。

paragraphs.en: Dilara Fındıkoğlu’s “Earthly Paradise” dress, from 2023.Photograph by Paul Westlake / Courtesy Metropolitan Museum of Art
paragraphs.zh: 来自2023年的Dilara Fındıkoğlu《尘世天堂》礼服。摄影：Paul Westlake / 由大都会艺术博物馆版权所有。

paragraphs.en: The title of “Costume Art,” a sprawling, stuffed-to-the-seams exhibition at the Metropolitan Museum of Art (running now through January, 2027), offers little clue as to what lies inside, other than, of course, clothing and art—or is it clothing that is art? There is no snappy subtitle to explain the show to the masses, as with previous exhibitions at the Costume Institute, such as “Heavenly Bodies: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination” and “PUNK: Chaos to Couture.” Nor is there a marquee name to lure in the crowds, as with “Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty” and “Charles James: Beyond Fashion.” This lack of bombast is actually a power move. Those past shows, glorious as they were to behold, sound, in retrospect, too eager to entice. “Costume Art,” by contrast, is an exhibition whose creators are confident that it will be seen, if not because of what it contains then simply because of where it is. The show marks the début of the institute’s brand-new Condé M. Nast Galleries, a nearly twelve-thousand-square-foot, five-room space situated just off the Met’s central Great Hall—a prime slice of real estate formerly inhabited by a gargantuan gift shop.
paragraphs.zh: “服装艺术”这个题目——这场在大都会艺术博物馆展出的规模宏大、内容铺陈得满满当当的展览（将持续到2027年1月）——除了一眼就能看见的服装与艺术之外，并没有提供更多线索。它到底是“服装”还是“艺术中的服装”？与以往服装研究所的展览不同，例如“HEAVENLY BODIES: Fashion and the Catholic Imagination”（《天体：时尚与天主教想象》）和“PUNK: Chaos to Couture”（《朋克：从混乱到高级定制》），这次没有一个醒目的副标题来向大众解释展览内容；也没有像“Alexander McQueen: Savage Beauty”（《Alexander McQueen：野性之美》）和“Charles James: Beyond Fashion”（《Charles James：超越时尚》）那样的“明星”名号来吸引人群。这样的低调其实是一种有力的策略。那些过去的展览，尽管令人叹为观止，但回头看却显得太过急于吸引人眼球。相比之下，“服装艺术”是由其策展人自信推出的一场展览：即使不是因为里面有什么，它也注定会被看到——只因它所处的位置。展览标志着该研究所在全新建立的康迪·M·内斯特画廊（Condé M. Nast Galleries）的首秀，这是一个约占地近一万二千平方英尺、五个房间的新空间，坐落在大都会艺术博物馆中心大礼堂旁边——这是一个价值极高、过去曾被一间庞大礼品店占据的展位。

paragraphs.en: The galleries (which are named for the founder of this magazine’s parent company) took three years to complete, but they represent more than eight decades of jockeying for legitimacy within the larger institution. The Costume Institute’s main dedicated galleries previously languished in the basement, and the department is responsible for raising most of its own budget each year, a task accomplished in large part by its annual fund-raiser, the starry Met Gala. Under the leadership of the curator Andrew Bolton—and with the patronage of Anna Wintour, the global editorial director of Vogue, who has used her influence to expand the department’s power—the institute has become a major force within the museum and a recognizable brand outside it. Of the ten most visited exhibitions in the Met’s history, five were put on by the Costume Institute. In 2018, “Heavenly Bodies” became the most attended Met show of all time. Now, as Bolton writes in the catalogue for “Costume Art,” the new galleries “situate fashion within the spatial and symbolic epicenter of the Museum.”
paragraphs.zh: 这些画廊（以本杂志母公司的创始人命名）耗时三年才建成，但其背后承载着该机构内长期八十多年争取合法性和地位的角力。时装研究所的主要专属展厅过去长期被放在地下室，而该部门每年要自行筹集大部分预算，这一任务很大程度上靠其年度筹款活动“星空大都会慈善会”完成。在馆长安德鲁·博尔顿（Andrew Bolton）的领导下，并得益于《Vogue》全球执行编辑安娜·温图（Anna Wintour）的资助与影响力——她运用个人影响力扩大了该部门的权力——该研究所已成为博物馆内部的一支重要力量，也成为馆外一个可识别的品牌。在大都会历史上访问人数最多的十场展览中，有五场由时装研究所举办。2018 年，“Heavenly Bodies（天体）”成为大都会历史上到访人数最多的展览。如今，正如博尔顿在《Costume Art》展览图录中写到的，那些新画廊“将时尚置于博物馆的空间与象征核心地位” 。

paragraphs.en: So why does “Costume Art,” ostensibly a victory lap, feel so defensive? The show may as well be called “Costume Is Art,” insomuch as it argues insistently that fashion, long derided as frivolous and decorative, can hold its own against any other art form—and, moreover, that the entire history of art can and should be viewed through the lens of clothing. Bolton makes this case by focussing on what he calls “the dressed body,” which he identifies as a central theme in every collection at the Met. Throughout the building, after all, you’ll find bodies “draped, wrapped, tailored, armored, incised, painted, ornamented, and otherwise fashioned,” Bolton writes. (Even when a body is naked, he notes, it is still cloaked in “physical and cultural ideals.”) As evidence of this ubiquity, he has paired each of the roughly two hundred garments in the show with a thematically resonant work of art from another Met department. The fashion exhibition doubles as a museum tour in miniature.
paragraphs.zh: 那么，为什么“Costume Art”这场表面上应是胜利巡礼的展览却显得如此防御性？这场展览也可以叫作“Costume Is Art”，因为它反复主张：长期以来被当作轻浮和装饰性艺术的时尚，完全可以与任何其他艺术形式平起平坐；更进一步，整个艺术史都可以也应当通过服装这一视角来理解。博尔顿的论证聚焦于他所称的“着装之身”，并把它视为大都会每一个藏品中的核心主题。毕竟，在整个建筑中，你会看到人体“被披覆、包裹、裁剪、铠化、切割、涂绘、装饰，或以其他方式被塑造”，博尔顿写道。（他还指出，即使是裸体的身体，也仍然被“身心和文化理想”所笼罩。）作为这种无处不在的证据，他把展览中约两百件服装中的每一件都与大都会其他馆系中的一件主题上共鸣的艺术作品配对，因而这场时尚展也成了一个微型的博物馆导览。

paragraphs.en: It begins, cleverly, with a section called “Naked and Nude Body.” In an act of pure showmanship, Bolton has placed mannequins clothed in fleshlike skintight garments in a tall glass vitrine visible to museumgoers heading toward the medieval wing—a seductive preview that may help lure them away from Arms and Armor. Bolton also establishes a format that continues through much of the exhibition: a line of mannequins standing high above their paired art works, signifying, in unsubtle terms, that, here, fashion is meant to dominate the discourse. An arresting sheer dress by the young Turkish British designer Dilara Fındıkoğlu—which uses snaking tendrils of synthetic hair to cover the wearer’s genitals, à la Botticelli’s Venus—towers over an eighteenth-century bronze Venus. A Pierre Cardin ensemble from 1967, featuring a dress with holes cut out to expose the breasts, looms atop a Flemish bust of Minerva preparing for battle in breast-baring armor. Bolton calls this technique of physically elevating the garments “pedestalizing.” Ironically, though, the gesture has the effect of undermining the clothes: the art works sit comfortably just below eye level, whereas the visitor has to crane her neck to take in the fashion designs above.
paragraphs.zh: 展览机智地从“裸体与裸身”这一单元开始。作为纯粹的展陈技巧，博尔顿把穿着拟肉感紧身衣的假人体模型，放在一座高挑的玻璃展柜内，摆在前往中世纪馆的人流路径上方——这是一次颇具诱惑力的预览，可能会引导观众离开“军械与盔甲”馆区。博尔顿也在展览中确立了一种模式：一排排人模高高站在与其搭配的艺术作品之上，用不含糊的方式表明，在这里时尚本该主导叙事。年轻的土耳其裔英裔设计师迪拉拉·芬迪库卢（Dilara Fındıkoğlu）的一件醒目透纱长裙，以蠕动的合成毛发卷曲线条遮住穿着者的生殖部位，宛如波提切利的维纳斯，屹立在一尊18世纪的青铜维纳斯雕像之上。1967年的皮耶尔·卡尔丹（Pierre Cardin）服装组合，里面有一件在胸部开孔的连衣裙，挺立在一尊披着露胸盔甲、正备战的弗拉芒版密涅瓦胸像之上。博尔顿将这种把服装在物理上抬高的手法称为“底座化”。但具有讽刺意味的是，这一姿态反而削弱了服装本身：艺术作品正舒适地位于视线下方，而参观者却必须仰头才能看清上方的服装设计。

paragraphs.en: A Thom Browne dress features a skeleton made of tiny beads.Photograph by Paul Westlake / Courtesy Metropolitan Museum of Art
paragraphs.zh: 一件托姆·布朗的连衣裙搭配了由细小珠串组成的骨架。保罗·韦斯特莱克（Paul Westlake）拍摄，来自大都会艺术博物馆提供。

paragraphs.en: Some of the pairings can be quite on the nose. In the “Classical Body” section, a golden Issey Miyake breastplate and bodice, molded to the curvature of the chest, is paired with a golden Etruscan cuirass. A row of Grecian-inspired draped gowns is paired with a row of Grecian urns featuring berobed ancients. These couplings are so literal that they seem to undercut Bolton’s thesis, suggesting that fashion raids art history rather than illuminating it anew. The following section, “Abstract Body,” does a more persuasive job highlighting fashion’s innovations, showing an array of corsets, panniers, and hoop skirts that designers used to manipulate the body into unnatural forms. Looking at a nineteenth-century corset that cinched the wearer’s waist into the circumference of a paper-towel roll, I felt, for the first time during the show, conscious of being in my own body (which squirmed at the thought of such sartorial constraints). The collection of elaborately engineered bustles and crinolines certainly advances the idea that clothing, even at its most punishing, can be a form of living sculpture. I felt a similar jolt in the “Reclaimed Body” section, which focusses on more avant-garde modifications, such as Rei Kawakubo’s bulbous creations with Comme des Garçons, three of which are paired with abstract sculptures that echo the garments’ austere curves. Each of the sculptures—by Max Weber, Henry Moore, and Jean Arp—was created prior to Kawakubo’s fashion experiments, but one gets a disorienting sense that, rather than Kawakubo reaching backward for touchstones, the sculptors were somehow anticipating her singular inventions.
paragraphs.zh: 有些搭配确实有些“说到做到”了。在“经典身体”单元中，一件塑形贴合胸部曲线的黄金伊赛·宫（Issey Miyake）胸甲与短上衣，与一件黄金埃特鲁里亚胸甲并置；一排受希腊启发的摺裹长裙，与一排描绘披着外衣的古人形象的希腊花瓶并置。这些配对过于字面，似乎在削弱博尔顿（Bolton）的论点，反而暗示时装是在“掠夺”艺术史，而不是为其重新照明。下一单元“抽象身体”则更有说服力地凸显了时装的创新，展示了各类紧身胸衣、拐子裙和伞裙，设计师们借助这些服装将身体塑造为不自然的形态。看着一件十九世纪的紧身胸衣——它把穿着者的腰围绞到几乎像卷纸巾般的周长时，我第一次在展览中有了明显的身体意识（想到这种穿衣束缚，自己的身体不由得有些本能地动了动）。精心构造的褶裥与伞裙装束展示，无疑进一步推进了这样的观念：服装即便在最苛刻的形态下，也可以成为一种“活体雕塑”。在“再生身体”单元，我也有了类似的震撼。这里关注的是更前卫的改造方式，例如川久保玲在《Comme des Garçons》中的鼓胀造型作品，其中三件与抽象雕塑相配，那些雕塑以与服装同样克制的曲线形成呼应。每件雕塑——来自马克斯·韦伯（Max Weber）、亨利·摩尔（Henry Moore）和让·阿普（Jean Arp）——都早于川久保玲的时装实验诞生，但令人错位的感觉是：与其说川久保玲在向过去寻找参照，不如说这些雕塑家在某种程度上先于她预见了这些独特的发明。

paragraphs.en: Most of the mannequins have mirrored faces, a flourish that, according to the catalogue, is meant to create “a moment of communal visibility,” though the heads are tilted at awkward angles that discourage selfie snapping. Eighteen unique mannequins were created for the show, modelled off real and imagined people whose bodies don’t conform to conventional fashion-world ideals. The “Corpulent Body” section features a mannequin, cast from the body of the French model and singer Yseult, looking formidable in a scaled-up Dior “Bar” suit. “Disabled Body” includes a mannequin cast from the activist Sinéad Burke, who has dwarfism, wearing a Burberry trenchcoat that’s been cut short; it’s accessorized, winningly, with a hat made from one of the coat’s lopped-off sleeves. (Why, one wonders, couldn’t there have been such mannequins in “Classical Body”? Were no designers inspired by Greek statuary with belly rolls?) “Pregnant Body” displays a Gucci dress, by Alessandro Michele, with a pair of beaded fallopian tubes across the belly—a design that is meant as a nod to women’s rights but risks reducing the wearer to a walking reproductive system. I preferred a long gown made by the Greek designer Dimitra Petsa using her signature “wet look” technique, so that its mesh fabric clings like seaweed to the mannequin’s pregnant form.
paragraphs.zh: 大多数人体模特都有“镜像”面孔。根据展览目录，这是为了营造“群体可见的一刻”，但头部却被故意倾斜到尴尬的角度，不太方便拍照打卡。展览为此次展出专门创作了18个独特人体模特，取材于现实与想象中的人，他们的身体都不符合传统时尚界的标准。“肥硕身体”单元中，有一个以法国模特兼歌手Yseult的身体铸造的人体模特，她身穿加大号“迪奥（Dior）酒吧”套装，显得格外强悍。“残障身体”单元则有一件以活动人士Sinéad Burke的身体铸造的人体模特，她有侏儒症。她穿一件被裁短的Burberry风衣，并被非常巧妙地配上了一顶由这件外套裁去的袖子制成的帽子。（那为何没有人也在“经典身体”里做这类模特呢？难道没有设计师会被带着小腹褶皱的希腊雕塑所启发？）“怀孕身体”展示了一件由Alessandro Michele设计的Gucci连衣裙，裙身有一对串珠状输卵管形装饰，位于腹部。这一设计意在呼应女性权利，但也有把穿着者简化成行走中的“生殖系统”的风险。我更偏爱另一件：希腊设计师Dimitra Petsa用其标志性的“湿感”工艺制作的一件长裙。其网纱面料紧贴着人体模特的孕育体态，像海藻那样贴身攀附。

paragraphs.en: A Vetements ensemble from the show’s “Aging Body” section.Photograph by Paul Westlake / Courtesy Metropolitan Museum of Art
paragraphs.zh: 展览“老龄身体”单元中的一套Vetements服装。照片由Paul Westlake拍摄 / 由纽约大都会艺术博物馆提供

paragraphs.en: The back half of the exhibit is smaller and more intimate, and it finally brings the clothing down off the pedestals, to ground level. “Costume Art” has no clear foot-traffic pattern. I learned only later that I was supposed to end at “Epidermal Body” (showcasing garments that mimic the look of skin), rather than at “Mortal Body,” which seemed like a more natural conclusion. Regardless, the latter portion of the show was by far the more affecting, not only because of its visceral themes—including tattoos, blood, and aging—but also because it offered a chance to admire exquisite garments up close. There’s a crimson Yohji Yamamoto dress made of silk crêpe de Chine, painstakingly manipulated into folds; a Yuima Nakazato piece that looks like bloody entrails dripping down the body; a Daniel Roseberry creation for Schiaparelli embellished with buttons in the style of Victorian mourning jewelry; and a Thom Browne dress featuring a pearly skeleton, made of seed-size beads, emerging out of the garment’s black surface like a spectre.
paragraphs.zh: 展览后半段规模更小、更私密，也终于把服装从基座上取下，放回到人的视线平面。“服装艺术”没有明确的人流动线。我后来才知道，我应该在“表皮身体”结束，而不是在“凡人身体”结束——后者似乎更自然一些。尽管如此，展览后段无疑更具冲击力，不仅因为它包含更具身体感官的主题——纹身、血迹、衰老——还因为它给了观众近距离欣赏精妙服装的机会。有一件朱莉·山本（Yohji Yamamoto）丝绉绸（crêpe de Chine）深红连衣裙，被细致地褶皱塑形；有一件Yuima Nakazato作品，看起来像血色的内脏从身体上滴落；有Daniel Roseberry为Schiaparelli创作的一件作品，以仿维多利亚时代丧服珠饰的服装扣饰为灵感；还有一件Thom Browne连衣裙，黑色衣面上生出由豆粒般小珠串成的珍珠质“骷髅”，仿佛幽灵般从服装中浮现。

paragraphs.en: Such pieces are undeniably art works—precious, impractical, one of a kind. The most attention-grabbing look in the show, though, might be one in “Aging Body”: a ready-to-wear Vetements ensemble featuring an absurdly oversized gray hoodie emblazoned with the message “I’M RETIRED (This is as dressed up as I get).” I watched as visitor after visitor snapped a photo of the sweatshirt, and I overheard more than one mumble about wanting to buy one. (They can do so, online, for seven hundred and eighty dollars.) This is presumably what “Costume Art” is fighting against—the idea that fashion exhibitions are, in essence, a vaunted form of window-shopping. And yet, as I watched people smile and laugh upon spotting the hoodie, I wondered what was so wrong with that idea. People love to fantasize about clothes, to imagine what their own bodies might feel like encased in finery or outfitted in some outré style. This, more than anything, is the impulse that has made the Costume Institute a hit. Is fashion art? Of course it is. But the clothes—the clothes are the thing. ♦
paragraphs.zh: 这些作品无疑都属于艺术品——珍贵、实用性不强、独一无二的。展览中最吸引眼球的造型，也许是在《老去的身体》中看到的一套成衣级别的 Vetements 搭配：一件夸张地过大的灰色连帽衫，上面印着“我已经退休了（这是我能穿得最正式的造型）”。我看到一位接着一位参观者都在给这件卫衣拍照，还听见不止一个人低声说想买一件。（他们可以在网上买到，价格是780美元。）这大概就是“服装艺术”想要反对的——即“时尚展览本质上只是被神化的橱窗购物”的观念。然而，当我看到人们一眼看见那件连帽衫就笑着、开心地反应时，我不禁想：这个观念到底错在哪儿？人们总爱对衣服进行想象，幻想自己的身体被华美衣饰包裹，或是穿上某种离奇的风格会是什么感觉。正是这种心理冲动让服装研究所一度成为热门话题。时尚是艺术吗？当然是艺术。可真正的焦点，是这些衣物——衣物本身。♦
